scope.summary: Implement MatCap material mode in ClothViewport as a dedicated fragment shader path that samples a MatCap texture using view-space normal UV coordinates, selectable via RenderMode::MatCap with a matcap_texture_ref in ViewportConfig
acceptance_criteria: A matcap_cloth.wgsl fragment shader samples a bound texture at UV coordinates derived from the view-space normal: uv = (view_normal.xy * 0.5 + 0.5)
ViewportConfig gains a matcap_texture_ref: Option<String> field referencing a 256x256 PNG artifact
RenderMode enum gains a MatCap variant; tailor_viewport_config accepts render_mode: 'matcap'
A default clay MatCap texture (neutral gray sphere with soft shading) is seeded as a system artifact on first launch
tailor_capture_frame with render_mode: 'matcap' returns a TailorVisualCapture showing MatCap shading
MatCap mode compiles and runs when the toon-shader feature is disabled (MatCap is independent of the Toon pipeline)
proof_targets: HBR-VIS: capture a draped dress in MatCap mode using the clay texture; assert output shows smooth shading variation without PBR specular highlights
scope.risk_if_missed: Without MatCap, clay-look QA passes (used to evaluate silhouette and drape shape without texture distraction) require a custom single-color PBR material with zero roughness and no normal map. This is a manual workaround with inferior results. MatCap is a standard tool-quality QA render mode.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
